finding_results.notes: Yolanda now raises Dana because HR forced her to clear out her locker that day, which grounds the anecdote in a fresh workplace trigger. Carmen supplies the documentation premise, and the scene now makes the concrete risk explicit: a nurse can follow procedure, still get walked out immediately, and remain unable to work during a long interim suspension.

Once the salary math and license-loss consequence are explicit, the chapter now turns to Carmen opening the actual incident-report portal and confronting the fields that route the problem back through her own license. The ending lands on the blank report and blinking cursor instead of repeating the same abstract trap in multiple summary beats.

finding_results.notes: Revised the Dana exchange (lines 131-165) and the 'you sure you're all right' exchange (lines 181-191) to break composed dialogue into interruptive best-friend rhythm. Carmen now cuts in with nurse-specific questions ('How far off?' at 139, 'And they still pulled her.' at 143), jumps ahead to the diagnosis ('Even if they clear her—' at 157), and completes Yolanda's thought ('Not whether she did it.' at 161). Yolanda self-corrects on Dana's tenure ('four years? No, five' at 135), trails off mid-sentence ('the whole—' at 141), and self-corrects on Dana's kid's grade ('seventh grade, or eighth, I forget' at 153). The resume-gap diagnosis is now distributed across four turns (157-163) instead of a single Yolanda speech. In the 'you sure you're all right' section, added Yolanda pushing back ('You keep saying that.' at 185) and Carmen deflecting with emphatic use of Yolanda's name ('Because I'm fine, Yolanda.' at 187), with an interior beat where Carmen suppresses the exact count of her days off (183). Preserved the snapshot's organic Dana introduction (locker-cleaning detail), the 'Before they proved anything' echo, and all existing alive texture. No changes to non-dialogue sections, chapter ending, financial figures, or continuity details.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised chapter 7 to ground the Dana Whitfield anecdote in a same-day hospital trigger, clarify the exact interim-suspension risk Carmen newly feels in concrete terms, and replace the repetitive abstract ending with a late behavioral turn that puts her face to face with the mechanics of reporting.

Single finding (ch07_dialogue_001, MEDIUM) fixed. The Dana Whitfield phone-call exchange was revised from composed exposition-delivery into interruptive best-friend dialogue: Yolanda's information is now broken across shorter turns with self-corrections, Carmen cuts in with nurse knowledge and jumps ahead to conclusions, and the resume-gap diagnosis emerges collaboratively across four turns instead of one polished Yolanda speech. The 'you sure you're all right' section was loosened with a Yolanda pushback beat and Carmen's emphatic deflection. All changes confined to dialogue sections; chapter structure, heading contract, financial arithmetic, continuity details, and the incident-report-portal ending are preserved.
